items.name: Purple Doomspike Helmet
items.description:
Forged in the heart of a dying star, this helmet gleams with a deep, cosmic purple hue. The front is adorned with shimmering stars that seem to shift and twinkle with an otherworldly light, while menacing spikes encircle it like a crown of shadows. Those who don the Purple Doomspike Helmet command the void, their presence a testament to the infinite mysteries of the night sky.

items.name: INSTRUMENT HELMET BASE
items.description:
Whistles, missiles, the miles make trials and sanity
Frays at the edge where the war drums echo vanity.
Steel rains down where the skies once sang,
Footsteps lost in a world gone bang.

Ash coats dreams, in dust they lie,
Questions scream but none reply.
Yet still we march, with hearts grown numb,
To whistles, missiles; the silence to come.

Carefully crafted. Made by inhuman hands.


2 part set, must be combined with a visor.

items.name: Nyst Øsvåling 1323 Steel Helmet
items.description:
Originally an Istvakyan design, the Øsvåling 1323 - christened as such in Nyst service - was the premier helmet of Nystyy's military well into the 15th century. Consisting of a steel shell and simple (initially leather) suspension system, it was designed to provide protection primarily against artillery fragments - although later production runs would experimentally attempt to improve protection against small arms. Thanks to the design's immense simplicity, such helmets are an incredibly common sight across less-industrialised minor nations like Nystyy, and have since proliferated into the hands of civilian collectors.

During the Twilight War, the Øsv.23 was used prolifically by Nyst forces - particularly so in static positions - becoming something of a symbol of the Nyst armed forces, so much so that its design lives on in ceremonial use to this day.
